title: deadline control
excerpt: Hello, i have a question. Is there possible to set a deadline for a task for example 4 p.m. and if time pass deadline user wont be able to continue process for example change stage and etc. this is very important case fo…

posts.author: lizi (lizi)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, i have a question. Is there possible to set a deadline for a task for example 4 p.m. and if time pass deadline user wont be able to continue process for example change stage and etc. this is very important case for us so please let me know. thank you in advance

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: You could get kind of close with some automation/workflow work. ie You could have an automation that changes a status column to ‘Locked’ (you could call it what you want) once a date is reach IF another status is not marked as complete. Then you could have automations set up that if a status is changed and the other status is ‘locked’, then XYZ. Could be, change the status that they changed back to original OR could be to notify the user that they changed the status when they weren’t supposed to.
